examples.title: Rotate Point About Origin
examples.description: Algorithm to rotate a single point (x,y) by angle θ about the origin using the rotation transformation formulas.
examples.explanation: This algorithm applies the standard 2D rotation transformation. The formulas x' = x cosθ - y sinθ and y' = x sinθ + y cosθ are derived from trigonometric addition formulas. The angle must be converted to radians as most programming languages use radian measure for trigonometric functions.

examples.title: Rotate Polygon About Origin
examples.description: Algorithm to rotate all vertices of a polygon by angle θ about the origin.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM RotatePolygon(vertices[], theta)
// vertices is array of (x,y) coordinates
// theta is in degrees
BEGIN
 radian ← theta × (π / 180)
 cos_theta ← cos(radian)
 sin_theta ← sin(radian)
 
 FOR i ← 0 TO length(vertices) - 1 DO
 x ← vertices[i].x
 y ← vertices[i].y
 
 // Apply rotation transformation
 x_new ← x × cos_theta - y × sin_theta
 y_new ← x × sin_theta + y × cos_theta
 
 vertices[i] ← (x_new, y_new)
 END FOR
 
 RETURN vertices
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm iterates through all vertices of a polygon and applies the rotation transformation to each. Computing cosθ and sinθ once before the loop improves efficiency. The time complexity is O(n) where n is the number of vertices.

examples.title: Rotate Point About Pivot
examples.description: Algorithm to rotate a point (x,y) about an arbitrary pivot point P(h,k) by angle θ using composite transformation.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM RotateAboutPivot(x, y, h, k, theta)
// Rotate point (x,y) about pivot (h,k) by angle theta
BEGIN
 radian ← theta × (π / 180)
 cos_theta ← cos(radian)
 sin_theta ← sin(radian)
 
 // Step 1: Translate so pivot moves to origin
 x1 ← x - h
 y1 ← y - k
 
 // Step 2: Rotate about origin
 x2 ← x1 × cos_theta - y1 × sin_theta
 y2 ← x1 × sin_theta + y1 × cos_theta
 
 // Step 3: Translate back
 x_new ← x2 + h
 y_new ← y2 + k
 
 RETURN (x_new, y_new)
END
examples.explanation: This implements the T-P-R method: Translate pivot to origin, Rotate, Translate back. Each step can also be represented as matrix multiplication in homogeneous coordinates: [x' y' 1] = T(h,k) × R(θ) × T(-h,-k) × [x y 1]

examples.title: Rotation Matrix Generation
examples.description: Function to generate the 2x2 rotation matrix for a given angle θ.
examples.code:
FUNCTION GetRotationMatrix(theta)
// Returns 2x2 rotation matrix
BEGIN
 radian ← theta × (π / 180)
 cos_theta ← cos(radian)
 sin_theta ← sin(radian)
 
 // Return rotation matrix [cos -sin; sin cos]
 matrix[0][0] ← cos_theta
 matrix[0][1] ← -sin_theta
 matrix[1][0] ← sin_theta
 matrix[1][1] ← cos_theta
 
 RETURN matrix
END

// Using the matrix to transform point
ALGORITHM TransformWithMatrix(matrix, x, y)
BEGIN
 x_new ← matrix[0][0] × x + matrix[0][1] × y
 y_new ← matrix[1][0] × x + matrix[1][1] × y
 RETURN (x_new, y_new)
END
examples.explanation: This separates matrix generation from transformation, allowing the same matrix to be reused for multiple points. This is more efficient when rotating many points with the same angle. The matrix is orthogonal with determinant +1.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per point, O(1) for matrix generation
